summary:
The afferent nerve fibers in the dental pulp originate from the trigeminal nerve (V2/V3 branches), specifically from cell bodies in the trigeminal ganglion, and transmit mechanosensitive and thermally sensitive sensations—functions distinct from autonomic nervous system activity. Since all provided options mistakenly assign these afferents to autonomic pathways such as sympathetic or parasympathetic fibers—which are efferent and involved only in modulation without sensing—all described choices are clinically inaccurate; thus, no option accurately reflects the true origin or functional characteristics of pulp afferent fibers. 

Note: This summary focuses solely on core anatomical facts directly tied to the query regarding afferent fibers’ identity and physiological roles within dentistry.
